text: The surgeon initiates the procedure by mobilizing the sigmoid and descending colon from a lateral to medial approach, using surgical instruments to carefully separate the colon from surrounding tissues.
The surgeon continues the cephalad dissection towards the splenic flexure, advancing the separation of tissue layers in this region.
The surgeon ligates the inferior mesenteric artery after achieving adequate mobilization of the colon, using robotic assistance and trocars placed in the left lateral abdomen and ostomy site.
The surgeon uses a vessel sealer to ligate vessels down to the level of the rectum, ensuring hemostasis in the area.
The surgeon confirms adequate mobilization of the colon, visually assessing its position and tension, before proceeding with the transection of the rectum.
The surgeon uses a grasper to secure the proximal colon through a 12mm trocar site.
The surgeon uses a Proline suture to mark the rectum, preparing for future colostomy reversal.
